title: Device Analytics
description: Analyze click and engagement data by device type (desktop, mobile, tablet) to understand how users interact with your campaigns across different devices.

steps.title: Understand device analytics
steps.description: Device analytics shows how your audience engages with campaigns across different device types (desktop, mobile, tablet). This data comes from the `deviceClicks` JSON field in `aggregateData` on CampaignOrder.

steps.title: Query device click data
steps.description: Fetch device breakdown data for campaigns. This query works with all permission levels.
steps.tip: deviceClicks is a JSON field containing click counts by device type (mobile, tablet, desktop, etc.).

steps.title: Understand deviceClicks structure
steps.description:
The `deviceClicks` JSON typically contains:

- **desktop**: Click count from desktop devices
- **mobile**: Click count from mobile devices
- **tablet**: Click count from tablet devices

Use these values to calculate device distribution percentages.

steps.title: Query multiple campaigns for device data
steps.description: Get device analytics across multiple campaigns with filters matching Dashboard usage.
steps.tip: Filter by status_in: LIVE for active campaigns, PAST for finished campaigns.

steps.title: Filter device data by date range
steps.description: Filter campaigns by date range to analyze device trends over a specific period.
steps.tip: Use startDate_gte and endDate_lte for date range filtering. The Dashboard defaults to 6 months of data.

steps.title: Combine with session data
steps.description: For deeper session-level device analysis, query attributed sessions.
steps.tip: Session data provides detailed user journey information by device.

steps.title: Optimize based on device data
steps.description:
Use device analytics to inform your campaign strategy:

- **High mobile traffic**: Ensure landing pages are mobile-optimized
- **Low tablet engagement**: Consider adjusting tablet bid modifiers
- **Desktop dominance**: B2B campaigns often see higher desktop engagement

Compare durationInSeconds and pageViews across device types to understand engagement quality.
steps.tip: Mobile users typically have shorter sessions but higher volume.

tips: Common status values: LIVE, LIVE_PENDING, LIVE_APPROVED, PAUSED, PAUSED_PENDING, PAUSED_APPROVED, APPROVED, PAST, DRAFT, LAUNCHING_LIVE, LAUNCHING_PAUSED
deviceClicks is a JSON scalar - parse it in your application code
Mobile users typically have shorter sessions but higher volume
Desktop users often have longer session durations and more page views
Use device data to optimize landing page experiences for each device type
Compare conversion rates across device types by joining with transaction data
Use orderBy: startDate_DESC to show most recent campaigns first
